message:
perf(connection): split concurrent context for direct DML fast path

The direct B-tree INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE paths in prepared-statement
execution were calling `concurrent_exec_context()`, which builds the
full `ConcurrentExecContext` — session id, shared handle, lock table,
commit index, busy timeout, AND a cloned rowid-allocator Arc plus a
schema-cookie read. Those last two pieces are only needed by the VDBE
rowid-allocation path; the direct DML fast path threads its own
allocation logic and never touches them, but it was paying the
allocation cost on every row.

This commit factors the work into three pieces:

- `concurrent_session_handle()` — the gating logic and one-time
  session/handle resolution. Returns `None` immediately when not in a
  concurrent transaction so callers stay branch-light. Both contexts
  build on top of it.
- `concurrent_page_io_context()` (new) — produces a slim
  `ConcurrentPageIoContext` containing only what the direct DML paths
  actually use: session id, shared handle, lock table, commit index,
  busy timeout. No allocator clone, no schema-cookie read.
- `concurrent_exec_context()` — unchanged shape, now thin wrapper
  around `concurrent_session_handle()` that adds the allocator/schema
  bits the VDBE path needs.

Three callers in the prepared direct DML fast path
(`run_prepared_direct_insert`, `run_prepared_direct_update`,
`run_prepared_direct_delete`) switch from `concurrent_exec_context()`
to `concurrent_page_io_context()`. The struct field set used by those
paths' `SharedTxnPageIo` wrapper is identical for both contexts so no
downstream change is needed.

Net effect: per-row direct DML in concurrent transactions drops one
`Arc` clone and one schema-cookie load. The VDBE path is bytewise
unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
